programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: A 90-year-old sings the Frank Sinatra classic that got him his voice back after a stroke. A 15-year-old sings about growing up Black in Britain. And father and son perform a lullaby.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George returns to help people make period homes fit for modern life. In West Yorkshire, Pip is moving back into the home that she grew up in and is finding it emotional.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers are back, as they explore more exceptional sights in the north of England. At the Northumberland County Show, they lend a hand prepping the animals.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: Theres a change in direction for Andy and Jules Spanish cycle adventure business. On the Costa Cálida, mini golf course owner Ian launches a new tournament.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners help Trevor and Asia find a place big enough for all their travel treasures. Can the Hamiltons find Gene and Theresa a place with a bedroom that passes the shimmy test?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tracey and Stephen from Staffordshire would love to make a permanent move to Spain. Theyve asked Ben Hillman to help them find their dream home in the province of Malaga.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Corfu holds a special place in Lisa and Waynes hearts, as its where they just got married. Can Laura Hamilton find them the perfect holiday home in Kassiopi for their £130k?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Terry and Ashley rush to finish the garden before visitors arrive. Will Alison and Zions flat plans come crashing down? Kathryn and Steve race to finish a medieval kitchen.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back on Sarah and Ian Renards two-bed rural renovation in Brixworth that didnt compromise on character.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: DJ Dom kicks off the week serving up a menu inspired by the 90s Madchester music scene. Hosting his first ever dinner party, Dom is a disaster in the kitchen and has to call on a chart-topping celebrity pal to bail him out.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Daddys girl Sasha is hoping to wow the guests with a Chinese/Pakistani fusion menu, but after revealing she hates the sound of cutlery on crockery, will chatty nurse Katherine find plastic cutlery and plates too much?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third day of this new competition in Manchester and the turn of nurse Katherine to host. Katherines going for an easy menu but hopes her starter of smoked salmon with a horseradish cream, and chorizo and chickpea main will still impress.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fourth day of the competition in Manchester and the turn of redcoat turned barber Danny to host. Dannys throwing an evening of Bulgarian cuisine in homage to his wife, but can he pull it off?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night in Manchester, and after a hit and miss week, glamour goth Maria is hoping to wow her guests with a traditional Asian meal with a twist.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: On the French Riviera, Paul takes control of a steam engine on the stunning Pine Cone line and rides in the cab of millionaire businessman Paul Ricards private line.

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, Amelia Dimoldenberg, Sarah Beeny and Maxine Peake take cupcakes to a new level, before recreating memorable romantic dates in biscuit form.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: A new group head for Virgin Island. To confront the issues holding them back from achieving intimacy in their lives, the participants unpack their deepest fears and anxieties.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The course content escalates. The group explore turn-ons and surrogate partner therapy. Alex gets to grips with his erection issues. And Marianne works on her reluctance to trust.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the Taskmaster in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the weeks biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Tissy is on the trail of a stolen 4x4. There is more van trouble in South Yorkshire as a dodgy driver tries to escape the pursuit specialists and a lad with no insurance gets more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: The Outcast
programme.desc: Jet Cosgrave has been swindled out of his fathers ranch by a wicked uncle. He returns eight years later with a bunch of gunslingers headed by the psychopathic Duke Rankin, and sets about plotting his revenge. Distractions, in the comely shapes of naïve ranch girl Judy Polsen and Alice Austin, his uncles fiancee, cannot deter Jet from the final showdown, but not before the two sides have already shed a lot of blood in dirt.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: In North Yorkshire, the traffic cops aim to protect lorry drivers and their cargo from organised gangs that target goods and trucks. In one case, a hijack is attempted on an HGV carrying £1 million in goods.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: On a busy Bank Holiday weekend, a day trip to Scarborough turns into a high-speed pursuit and a desperate suspect on a motorbike puts lives at risk when a passenger falls off.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The cops deal with a major drugs bust in North Yorkshire, a wanted man linked to an alleged assault on a woman, and a gang of suspected poachers who are caught driving an untaxed van.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sergeant Mick and the Operational Support Unit carry out a raid on a house with suspected drug activity, and uncover a cannabis farm in the loft. There is motorcycle madness as Gav apprehends a biker risking his and other road users lives.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptor Mick leads a team raiding two flats whose occupants are suspected of producing and selling spice. Duncan is involved in a high-speed pursuit, which ends with tracker dog Tia on the escaping offenders trail.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A runaway driver flees an accident in a decommissioned ambulance, then crashes through a level crossing and ditches his ride on a live railway line. While a chopper tracks the suspect, cops frantically battle to close the railway line.

programme.title: Shoplifters and Scammers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: Documentary charting methods used to thwart criminals targeting shops, beginning with a private crimes team employed to catch shoplifters in Londons West End.

programme.desc: The doctors of Balham Park Surgery treat victims of accidents, including a young girl whose hair was ripped out by a whisk, an elderly man who was involved in a hit-and-run, and a woman who fell off her moped.

programme.title: Rise of the Footsoldier 4: Marbella
programme.desc: Fresh out of prison, Pat Tate seeks revenge from the man who put him away. But once he arrives in Marbella, his simple mission becomes a complex series of problems as hes offered the drug deal of a lifetime and must get the cash he needs from Essex, via his unreliable friends Tony and Craig. When the money is stolen, Pat needs to pull off an audacious plan `B and get his team home in one piece.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A white van man makes a break for it after ploughing into a parked car, a suspect is not keen on wearing a set of cuffs, and the driver of a stolen car is brought to a halt thanks to some railings.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors

programme.desc: Brush decides to start a ladies club and invites all the ladies of the forest for brunch. When Fox and Tusk try to join in, dressed as ladies, Brush soon discovers their true identity and kicks them out. Offended, Fox and Tusk decide to start a club.

programme.title: Beathaichean a Bocadaich
programme.desc: Sophie and her friends dance like a fish and a gorilla.

programme.title: Meaban and Moo
programme.desc: Tha Meaban is Moo a cruthachadh pìos ealain an urra. Meaban and Moo are making some artwork.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha Meanbh Chlangair s a caraidean Lusach, a cluich ceòl còmhla ris na Craobhan Ciùil. Tiny Clanger and her flower friends are making music with the Music Trees.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha a Chearc Iarainn an-dòchas gum bi cuideigin ann a ghabhas a cuid sgudail. The Iron Chicken is hoping to find someone to take her rubbish.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Ruairidh Graham reads Rud Beag Misneachd.

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Grga has to earn some cash so he can fulfill his dream - flying in a hot air balloon.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian

programme.title: Seonaidh

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Were not short of water in Scotland and Calum is in the Highlands to find out more about hydroelectric power .

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: Willis Bottersnike costume fails to beat Tinks at the fancy dress party, so goes to Snike Hill to prove how convincing his outfit is. He ends up stuck in the Kings ear, where he poses as his conscience and begins instructing him on what to do.

programme.title: Bus Ghlaschú
programme.desc: The story of the Glasgow bus, a lifeline between Donegal and the Scottish city.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic

programme.desc: Anne meets the Rubilar family in Montreal. Originally from Chile, they are now searching for their great grandfather, Daniel Morrison from the Isle of Lewis.

programme.title: Fergie, The Ceilidh King
programme.desc: A dèanamh luaidh air am fear-ciùil Fergie Dòmhnallach à Loch Abar a chaochail na bu thràithe am-bliadhna.Tribute to Lochaber musician Fergie MacDonald who died this year.

programme.title: Dàna: Scotlands Wild Side
programme.desc: The mountain amphitheatre of Torridon is famed both for its spectacular winter environment and the rock climbing available in summer months. To gain a full understanding of this incredible area, Coinneach travels there at opposite ends of the year.

programme.title: Leonardo da Vinci - Deisciobal an Eòlais
programme.desc: Leonardo works as a military engineer, designs fanciful flying machines, studies light and shadow and investigates gravity. He also dissects cadavers and pens treatises on a vast array of subjects, all while seeking the perfect patron.

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Julie and Muireann are joined by singer Eilis Kennedy and concertinist Edel Fox as she visits Glasgow. There, they discover a city full of musical talent and meet Karen Matheson, Linda MacLeod, Ceitlin L.R. Smith and Simon Thoumire.

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Abadas

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh and Tilidh

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Chan eil maighdinnean-mara dha-rìribh - a bheil? Tha Art airson a cheist-sa fhuasgladh - fiù s ged a tha pioraidean anns an rathad. Mermaids dont exist, do they? Art wants to be able to answer this question.

programme.title: Na Speuran
programme.desc: Blast off into space with Astronaut Emily, who demonstrates how sound in space differs from here on Earth.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc

programme.title: SpeakGaelic

programme.desc: Bracaistean falainn a th aig na seòid is Ruairidh le trioblaidean cridhe. The lads focus on healthy breakfasts after Roddys heart condition diagnosis.

programme.title: Inside Chernobyls Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary which follows the construction of a trailblazing 36,000 tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 Chernobyl disaster. It films close up with the team of international engineers as they race to build the new structure before Chernobyls original concrete sarcophaguscollapses.

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Viewed by society as an embarrassment and an inconvenience, homeless alcoholic Edna is shunted from doss houses to hospitals, psychiatric wards, even prison. When she seems finally to find a stable environment, the house is forced to close.

programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the home insurance policies refusing damage claims if the cause can be linked to events that were insured under an old policy.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A plot of land, perfect for building on in Derby, has Dion excited, and the new owners are just as keen. Tommy is in Suffolk, at a projerct where the decorative plaster on the walls is going to be a big job for somebody to deal with. And Jacqui, also on a visit to the same county as Tommy, meets a retired butcher who has decided to turn his hand to property development.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp meets collector Dawn Austin, whose collection of more than 800 Barbie dolls takes up an entire room of her house. Meanwhile, Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon lead the reds and blues around a huge antiques centre in Heanor.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Crofters DJ and partner Lindsay want to turn their unloved breeze-block garage into a homely croft shop where they can sell their produce. The dull building has no character, so Banjo and his team work from scratch to transform this empty space.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple want to swap their London flat for something bigger in the East Midlands, where Nicki Chapman opens the front doors to a lovely selection of homes with lovely gardens. Nicki cant miss the opportunity to meet another young couple whove already made their escape to develop a luxury leather goods business. After all, Nicki does love a good bag!

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Scotlands Home of the Year
programme.desc: Interior designers Anna Campbell Jones and Banjo Beale and architect Danny Campbell travel the country in search of Scotlands best homes. In the west, the judges must choose between a Georgian town house in Ayr, a semi-detached house in Dumbreck and a Victorian villa in Kilmacolm. Scoring them on architectural merit, distinctive design and personal style, which home will Anna, Banjo and Danny judge worthy enough to go forward to the grand final?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: A new arrival on the farm means Andrew, Gayle and Terrence have even more to juggle.

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond, with members of the public putting their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most.

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Just as she thinks her blackmailers are under control, Gillian is stunned when a curveball is thrown her way. Meanwhile, an anxious Maggie carries out her risky plan to get proof of Vivs lies, Sam wears his heart on his sleeve when he opens up to Scarlett, and Amber is forced to face her fears.

programme.title: Jules and Gregs Wild Swim
programme.desc: Jules and Greg take the plunge into the Isle of Skyes dramatic rock pools and waterfalls.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Scotlands Greatest Escape
programme.desc: Judith Ralston and Grado are on the road exploring the often-overlooked Scottish Borders. They sample delights in the coastal town of Eyemouth, including a stay in a mansion built for a notorious smuggler. They travel to Kelso for some RandR in a beautifully restored 12th-century manor before heading to Newcastleton to check out the local agricultural show and stay in an off-grid alpine chalet.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Scotlands Greatest Escape
programme.desc: Judith Ralston and Grado sample Scotlands south coast in Dumfries and Galloway. The adventure starts at the historic Caerlaverock Estate, where Grado enjoys an overnight in a treehouse. They experience majestic birds of prey on the Galloway Red Kite Trail and spend the night in coastal glamping domes. Their final stop takes them to Portpatrick, where they stay in the Edwardian luxury of Dunskey Castle.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce, and Brian provides an update on the veg he sowed at the start of the series.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie heads for Ben Ledi in the Trossachs to help with some high-altitude fieldwork. And 25 years on from the foot-and-mouth outbreak, Arlene finds out about a legacy that is helping todays farmers.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: A single mum from Scotland embarks on a whirlwind romance with a Ghanaian preacher. They quickly marry, and she becomes the first lady of his church. But lives are torn apart when tragedy strikes.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Reporter Myles Bonnar travels to Ghana to look into Charmains death. There, he interrogates the police investigation that was carried out at the time and meets those who were at the scene as he searches for the truth about what happened in room 112.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Reporter Myles Bonnar runs the case by a veteran detective and investigates Eric Adusahs past, uncovering hidden truths. With so many questions about what happened to Charmaine, he tracks the Prophet down in search of answers.

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: The Bairds and Whytes arrive home from a pub quiz. Colin is miffed they only came third, mocking the winners for taking it far too seriously. Beth reminds him they were raising money for charity.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: T in the Park
programme.desc: Edith Bowman delves deep into the BBC archive to revisit stellar performances from T in the Park. Features an array of artists that appeared between 1994 and the festivals last year in 2016, including Ed Sheeran, Black Eyed Peas and Paolo Nutini.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Dark States
programme.desc: Milwaukee is said to be one of the most racially divided and impoverished cities in the US. With spiraling gun crime and homicide rates, this Midwestern city encapsulates Americas complex and troubled relationship with guns and the increasing disharmony between African American communities and the police.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the crew visit the Southern States of the USA in search of more predators to add to his Deadly list. He dives into the crystal clear waters of the Silver Springs River to swim with a wild Alligator and adds his first plant to the list, a carnivorous Venus Flytrap. He also feels the sting of the formidable Fire Ant and joins a team of scientists, tracking and tagging wild Bobcats.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a look at some of the super skills and senses that animals around the world use to hunt and survive. From the blistering speed and acceleration of the Cheetah to the elastic tongue and 360-degree vision of the chameleon and even a shark with a remarkable, hidden, sixth sense.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The nine designers storm a medieval-style castle to turn hotel bedrooms into perfect love nests as they draw on their own ideas of what makes a romantic retreat for inspiration. They are able to reuse and repurpose the hotels existing furniture to show off their ability to upcycle and demonstrate their styling skills, before they are judged by Michelle and hotel design expert Linda Boronkay.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.sub-title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott heads to the USA and the small college town of Moscow, Idaho to find out about the tragic murder of four students and the global online manhunt that followed their deaths.

programme.title: Candyman
programme.desc: Anthony, a visual artist, meets an old-timer who exposes the true story behind Candyman. Anxious to use these macabre details as fresh grist for paintings, he unknowingly unleashes a terrifying wave of violence.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey heads to Bedfordshire to the private site where 23-year-old Chantelle lives with her husband, Swayley. Chantelle prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her granny Rita taught her and sharing them on social media.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception - conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.sub-title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott heads to the USA and the small college town of Moscow, Idaho to find out about the tragic murder of four students and the global online manhunt that followed their deaths.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Forest Elephants: Rumbles in the Jungle
programme.desc: Andrea Turkalo has been studying the complex social lives and unique vocalisations of the Central Africas Forest Elephants, getting to know over 4,000 intimately. As new acoustic research sheds light on the many mysteries that still surround forest elephant society. Will these endangered elephants finally speak out and tell Andrea what it is they need to survive?

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The show comes from Dover Castle in Kent. Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to take off to auction. Catherine falls for a colourful brooch and Jonathan unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill. Paul Martin uncovers the story of Dover Castles secret tunnels.

programme.desc: Ruth Bader Ginsburg is a struggling attorney and new mother who faces adversity and numerous obstacles in her fight for equal rights. programme.title: Artemis: To the Moon and Back
programme.desc: The inside story of the Artemis II mission - filmed over three and a half years at NASA. How did four astronauts travel to the Moon, going deeper into space than ever before?

programme.title: Art Ninja

programme.title: Hey You What If?
programme.desc: Running a marathon is hard.At least it is for a human.But what if we could swap our legs with an animal - would that make it any easier? First, we try on a pair of kangaroo legs. Rather than jump from one leg to the other, kangaroos keep their legs together and store the energy from each bounce in special ligaments that act like springs. They recycle the energy so that the kangaroo can travel long distances with very little effort.

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steves on a mission to find dinosaurs that have something a little special: fearsome killers, with incredible abilities! Get ready for a sensory sensation! His quest sees him race in the dark like a Troodon, track prey from miles away taking tips from the Daspletosaurus, and finally he gets his hands on a real life Caiman to show how their pressure pits are just like that of the Spinosaurus. Strap yourself in for a sensory overload!

programme.title: Operation Ouch!

programme.title: Bluey

programme.desc: Nick is having a busy day when a very tired bee visits him and Norman in the caravan. Together with the Popcasters, they learn how important bees are to the planet, and they write a song to help the busy bee get its buzz back.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella plays with her favourite cup - it makes the perfect drum for a sing along with Lift. Together, they visit Kingsley and Queenie who play the Tea Party Dash, but who will win?

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing is at Ammas creche and it is his turn to choose the game, Musical Statues. Flop gets the Bingpad going and soon all the children are dancing like crazy. Sula goes out first, quickly followed by Coco who both watch for wobblers with Amma. In the final round, Bing wobbles at the last moment and Pando wins much to Bings distress - he chose the game so he thought he should win it. Fortunately, Charlie never stopped dancing.

programme.title: Yakka Dee

Dee comforts a friend with crying eyes, and comes across some superhero eyes. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dees friend finally succeeds in saying todays word.

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Lu is disappointed when she gets a second frog toy and Biba gets a brand new squeaky squid toy, and gets further upset when Biba refuses to swap toys with her.

programme.title: Twirlywoos

Chickedy and Chick get into the washing basket and the man accidentally pegs them up on the line too! The Twirlywoos are having tea, but the tea table is misbehaving. It keeps going further and further up, propelling Great BigHoo and Toodloo all the way out of the top of the boat!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Its showtime. Justin and his friends put on a play, whilst Mr Tumble has a go at making a puppet theatre, but managing all his actors proves harder than he thought.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Laa-Laa and Po pick delicious green beans. The Noo-Noo puts the beans into the Tubby Custard Machine and out comes tasty, green Tubby Custard.

programme.desc: Gemma, Benjee Bear and Bailee Bear welcome toddlers Addi, Elodie, Vincent, Arrella and Harry, and their grown-ups, to the Toddler Club. The Whats in the Box? game reveals a superhero mask, and when the toddlers and parents open up a bigger box, they discover lots more objects and toys that are inspired by superheroes.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Murphy is in the middle of a mega clean-up in the attic, but he cant find Rebeccas lost hat. His magic cleaning machine goes wrong and causes mayhem, but it does give him a monster make idea - a paper banana skin! The Mini Monsters create wonderful scrap bag monsters, while Rebeccas grandpa tries to create a new ice cream flavour and a dreamy creamy sundae. The Recycling Express brings an old box with a window, which Murphy and Rebecca transform into a terrific box truck.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: The Broccolisaurus needs a haircut. Luckily, their florets are a Pea-Rexs favourite snack.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey, Bingo and Mum get into a debate with Dad about the definition of teasing. As they recall all the times hes been a big teaser, Dad argues that he was only playing.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels decide they want to be superheroes. Is there anyone that needs their help?

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and friends embark on a great adventure, singing all about camping as they travel into the woods, learning about all the different joys of camping in the wild outdoors.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk along a canal to a place where they can blow bubbles. On the way, they discover a lift for boats, pretend to be pirates, spot some canal boats and even meet an octopus!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Mum and Dad have a treat in store for George and Freddie. Its a special tennis game including dinosaurs, bees, and smiley faces. Later, George and Freddie get the face paints out.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Balthazar is in the kitchen cooking a traditional Turkish revani cake, helped by his sister Serena and brother Ted. Coconut is a really important ingredient in his recipe, as it is put in the cake as well as on top. Balthazar invites his friends to taste the coconut before he cooks it, but they dont like it very much. He thinks hell change their minds when they taste his finished cake.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a barbers to find out how how clippers work to cut hair, and using a special microscope camera, Maddie shows us exactly how the blades work.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Kika is a golden labrador and a guide dog for her owner Dr Amit, who is blind, which means he cannot see. She is trained to use her super sight to help Dr Amit do as many things as he can by himself. Together, they make the best team! Dr Amit is showing his children Abhi and Anoushka how to read braille, a special pattern of clever dots that helps people to read using their fingers.

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet some wildebeest hanging out in a big group. The second animal they see, a monarch butterfly, has an astonishing wild connection with the wildebeest.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.desc: Maddie and Triggs get blown away and take to the skies on a magical, windy adventure.

programme.title: Ranger Hamzas Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how caddisflies play such an important role in the environment. They find out that caddisfly larvae, which are young caddisflies, live in rivers and make shelters for themselves by recycling what they find in the water. On the way, they look for caddisfly larvae in their shelters and learn how they also help keep the river clean!

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Elvis must learn to tie his own laces quickly to achieve his PB in goalkeeping.

programme.title: Graces Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, CBeebies very own motorbike rider, Grace, shows off three amazing machines that can go on land and water. Grace goes for a ride on a quad bike that turns into a jet ski, a vehicle that floats using its wheels and a pond weed cutter!

programme.title: Dodges Pup School
programme.desc: Dodge spots a mysterious door in the school hallway. The pups hear all kinds of exciting and curious noises coming from behind it, and their imaginations run wild. They have to find out what is inside.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster arrives at the Lost Things Office ready to help Elder Bunny try and reunite the citizens of Fluffytown with possessions they might have lost.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
programme.sub-title: The Octonauts and the Harlequin Duck
programme.desc: Sleepwalking mice are out of hibernation and in harms way unless the Octonauts can help; Calico Jack and Kwazii reunite a mommy duck with her eggs.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Tiny Terror
programme.desc: Peters plans to raid Mr McGregors garden are thwarted when he discovers the Shrew has got there first. Its down to the rabbits to find a way to get their garden back.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are close to finishing a jigsaw puzzle when the Naughty Mice steal their last piece. To get it back they must take part in a series of puzzles in an underground adventure set by the Puzzler.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: When the Mouse Family is exhausted, Vida realises it is because Baby Mouse is teething and crying all night! Luckily, she knows how to soothe Baby Mouse, so her family can get the rest they need.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato invites Evil Pea to stay with him at Tato Tower, but she soon takes it over.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasys turns detective when his harmonica disappears.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs Mojos racer top speed to get his new plane off the ground. But when his flight goes wrong, Mojo and Bo have to take to the air in Mojos jet top to help him down!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad tries to teach Bluey how to play chess, but Mum thinks she is still too young. When Bingo joins in, the kids make up their own rules, but they still cant beat Dad.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget attempts to make all her Piri friends secret `wishing tree hopes and dreams come true - but gets them the wrong way round, with funny and very unexpected consequences.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Chloe tries to teach her dad to improvise and play Blueys game of Octopus at home, he finds it hard to understand, until they realise they can play it their own way.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum uses Avas doubling machine by mistake, doubling all his recycling. But with Dr Ollies help, it becomes Cyclo the Recycling Robot, giving the children lots of fun.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: AJ takes Evie and Anaya swimming, but Waffle must stay at home with Simon. Refusing to miss out on the fun, Waffle begins an adventure of his own and sets off to find Evie.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi makes gingerbread people for the bakery but thinks they look messy next to Jakes neat ones. She wishes them to be perfect - and suddenly they spring to life! The cheeky gingerbreads dash through the cafe, splattering icing and riding on Deborahs back as they try to reach Gingerbread Land. With Jakes help, Rafi traps them under a cake dome. Dash reminds her that their messy look is what makes them special.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Spring: Its Time to Build a Nest
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran spot a birds nest in Cynthias garden, which inspires JoJo to make a nest for Panda. Cynthia and Gran Gran teach JoJo about all the nests that different birds make to help their babies. Gran Gran has a plan: while JoJo waits for the eggs they found to hatch, they can go to the park and see all the ducklings that have already hatched out and about! Meanwhile, Daddy helps teach JoJo how to whistle just like a bird.

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Sarah and Duck attempt to find out why their friend Donkey seems so sad.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small catches a space probe while fishing. Major says it is a useless thing but Mother thinks the probe might be visiting to say hello. This gives Major Clanger an idea!

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa find a floaty feather belonging to a Tweetie. This inspires an adventure across Planet BeddyByes, noticing floaty things such as feathers, bubbles and GoGo Balloon.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos trousers get mixed up all over the place - in the Ninky Nonk, in the Pinky Ponk. No matter how often the Tombliboos change their trousers back, it happens all over again.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A different story read by a special guest each night just before bedtime.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Following Martin and Ronees wedding and the birth of Niles and Daphnes child, Frasier is feeling lonely on his own in the flat. He makes a decision about the San Francisco job, but when he tells his family and Roz of his decision, there is a serious misunderstanding.

programme.desc: Children of the Crossfire: A look at how the recent surge in immigration raids in Minneapolis by ICE left children traumatised. Families face difficult choices and continue to live in fear.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north London and Hertfordshire, Dawn and Simon bond with hipsters Darren and Sian, but can any of them find common ground with multi-lingual lawyer Ayesha and astrophysicist Stephen?

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George takes on a 16th century listed Cotswold cottage with some nasty surprises. Theres also a Kent oast house needing a super cool bedroom for an eight-year-old, where he tries to make a disco ball and neon lights work with exposed oak beams.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The guys decide to `help Carla when she receives no responses to her personal ad.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Norms neighbour tells him Vera is having an affair.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The gang at Cheers calls in a ringer to help the pub win a bowling match against a rival bar.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gets upset after seeing Rays reaction to her friends breast enhancement operation.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra have an argument about their new can opener, and Marie, Frank and Robert cant help getting involved.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Frank makes everyone suspicious when he begins to show an interest in Rays work and acts fatherly.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Dr Frasier Crane returns to Seattle and a new job as a radio psychiatrist. However, his life is not destined to be quiet. His brother Niles, also a psychiatrist, is a dab hand at guilt-tripping and insists Frasier takes in their father - a man with whom he has nothing in common. So thats when the psychic Daphne makes an entrance to help out.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier is constantly interrupted when he tries to find a quiet place to read.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin turns the tables on Frasier and Niles when they decide to take him to dinner at a fancy restaurant. Afterwards, Niles meets Daphne for the first time.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier becomes the target of a hate campaign organised by a local newspaper columnist, so Martin and Daphne encourage him to fight back.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Ramsay heads to Maxs Bar and Grill to help the restaurant before the divorced owners business crumbles.

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4s midday news summary.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Shropshire, Simon OBrien turns a redundant telescope into an unusual light, while Henry Cole restores a vintage Italian moped to take for a spin.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers head to Kielder Forest, Englands largest man-made woodland. They see an outdoor art gallery with sculptures dotted across the lakeside paths. Turning their eyes to the skies, they get a glimpse of ospreys, and get up close with raptors.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Teacher Wendys looking for her dream holiday home in Puglia, southern Italy. Jean Johannson lines up five great properties for her £200k, but will any of them get top marks?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In France, Sophie and Will have their hands full at their Alpaca farm. In Spain, are Brian and Shirley about to throw in the towel at their cave house BandB?

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Ben and Billie battle brambles to create a riverside retreat. Louisa and Rogers new suite has last-minute design changes. Mark and Sandra scramble to ready their gîte for guests.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Rodriguezes help Karl and Penni embrace outdoor living. Will they find the wow factor? Can the Browns find a place that satisfies Veronica, Harvey and their teenage daughter?

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: Paul Merton: Driving Amazing Trains
programme.desc: From the Lakes to Whitby, Paul steams through the north of England on a narrow-gauge loco and a cruise on Windermere, and experiences Queen Victorias private carriage and a 50-tonne wartime loco.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the Taskmaster in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: Maggie cooks up a plan to get Eddie away from Whitney once and for all. But in classic Maggie fashion, she goes too far and breaks Eddie in the process.

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: After taking Eddie on a trip down memory lane, Maggies gobsmacked by Eddies shocking revelation. Maggie has already sacrificed so much to get Eddie back. Can she forgive this?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: A new group head for Virgin Island. To confront the issues holding them back from achieving intimacy in their lives, the participants unpack their deepest fears and anxieties.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: The course content escalates. The group explore turn-ons and surrogate partner therapy. Alex gets to grips with his erection issues. And Marianne works on her reluctance to trust.

programme.title: Imperfect Murder
programme.desc: Strube: Heather Strubes life took a tragic turn during a bitter custody battle with her ex-husband, Steven, when his mother pulled a fatal trigger. (S3, ep 8)

programme.title: I Met My Murderer Online
programme.desc: Deanna Ballman: Each episode tells the story of how an online connection led to murder. Every story is different, but every one has a tragic end.

programme.title: I Met My Murderer Online
programme.desc: Stories of how online connections led to murder. Whether its the excitement of online dating, the lure of money-making schemes, or the chance to cheat on your partner, every story is different, but every one has a tragic end.

programme.title: The First 48
programme.desc: A generous Atlanta man is murdered and Detective Egbert suspects he may have been a victim of his own kindness. In Oklahoma, a man is shot while driving down the street, and Sergeant Walker questions youths who tried to break into the victims house.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When a gunfight erupts at a local biker bar and claims the lives of two men, Lt. Joe Kenda must delve deep into the world of an infamous biker gang to determine if the cause behind the violent encounter was gang rivalry or something more personal.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: In his first case, rookie Joe Kenda volunteers to work a multi-victim shooting deemed to be unsolvable by the veteran detectives.

programme.title: Fugitives Caught on Tape
programme.desc: Law enforcement officers pursue fugitives on the run. Deputies race to extract a mother and her daughters from their truck after a fleeing suspect crashes into them. And fish and wildlife officers pursue a fugitive attempting to escape by swimming.

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A look at some of the strangest arrests from across the country: a desperate woman needs to poop during a traffic stop, a mum is busted sneaking into a school dressed as her daughter; a man jumps into a stores 24,000 gallon aquarium and more!

programme.title: My Strange Arrest
programme.desc: A hyped-up man goes toe to toe with police; a woman flips off fellow drivers while dancing in the street; a pilot is arrested for allegedly stealing a plane; and more.

programme.title: Court Cam
programme.desc: A convicted cop killer tells the court that he deserves the death penalty; a judge dismisses the jury after a witness testimony becomes too emotional; a man charged with DWI gets caught lying to the judge; and more.

programme.title: What the Killer did Next
programme.desc: A look at what a murderer chooses to do in the minutes, hours, and days after their crime. Homing in on the window of time between the murder of the victim and the capture of the killer.

programme.title: What the Killer did Next
programme.desc: Police thought Manchester mum Sian Robertss murder was a cut-and-dried case when they got a frantic 999 call from her ex. They couldnt have been more wrong.

programme.title: What the Killer did Next
programme.desc: Police are at a loss when a 79-year-old is killed in her Hartlepool home, but when the evidence mounts and the killers movements are tracked, the net closes in.

programme.title: Murder by Medic
programme.desc: Delving into the stories of killers who broke the Hippocratic Oath to use their medical knowledge for murder, taking the lives of often vulnerable victims instead of caring for them.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Crash Dive
programme.desc: Set aboard the USS Corsair, a submarine on active duty. Against his wishes, Ward Stewart is made executive officer and given just days to prepare. Before boarding he meets schoolteacher Jean Hewlett, and is captivated. He plans romance, unaware that she already has a sweetheart: Dewey Connors, commander of the Corsair and Stewarts boss. Will their rivalry be discovered in time?

programme.desc: Based on Henry James Washington Square. A handsome fortune hunter woos an unattractive but wealthy woman.

programme.title: Boston Strangler
programme.desc: Reporter Loretta McLaughlin becomes the first person to connect a series of murders and break the story of the Boston Strangler. She and Jean Cole challenge the sexism of the early 1960s to report on the citys most notorious serial killer.

programme.title: My Friend Dahmer
programme.desc: The true, haunting story of the young Jeffrey Dahmer, a shy adolescent in high school who becomes a notorious serial killer years later.

programme.title: The Night House
programme.desc: Reeling from the unexpected death of her husband, Beth (Rebecca Hall) is left alone in the lakeside home he built for her. She tries as best she can to keep together-but then the dreams come. Disturbing visions of a presence in the house call to her, beckoning with a ghostly allure. But the harsh light of day washes away any proof of a haunting. Against the advice of her friends, she begins digging into his belongings, yearning for answers.

programme.desc: Its all go as Rick celebrates the abundance of seafood and the rich colourful sauces of Naples, or Napoli, and visits Suffolk to experience North Sea cod with cabbage and beer.

programme.desc: Its home and away for seafood fan Rick as he catches turbot off the Cornish coast and cooks it with cider and cream, and travels to Goa for prawn caldene and spicy Goan lobster.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.sub-title: Ask Me Anything: All About Kids
programme.desc: Ree Drummond opens up in a fun QandA about kids, with some of the questions coming from her pint-sized fans. Her answers include an 11-carton cake and ravioli a la Bobbi, named after one of her young questioners.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares four recipes that can be on the table in less than 40 minutes, including chicken with pesto cream, Cajun veggie pasta, sheet pan shrimp puttanesca and skirt steak with bok choy.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver celebrates frozen ingredients as he creates a scrumptious fish pie, creamy cauliflower cheese spaghetti and a warming minestrone soup, all for under £1 per delicious portion.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve bakers started. Now just three remain but only one can win. Paul and Prue set three challenges that will test the bakers skills, including an intricate, multi-layered, edible landscape Showstopper that could win them the title.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Galton Blackiston, his pal and a top-class, Michelin-starred chef. They don diving suits to swim with seals, and hire an unusual mode of transport to take them around the islands. Galton visits a sea salt farm and delivers some back to James, who uses it to cook up some wonderful salt-baked sea bass. James finds an unusual restaurant situated in the middle of a post office, and finishes off the day cooking roast duck by a stunning seashore.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to one of his favourite places in the UK: the beautiful county of Cornwall. Joining him is his close pal and Michelin-starred chef Galton Blackiston. During their trip they discover some world-class Cornish ice cream, and Galton visits Newlyn fish market to pick up some Cornish cod. James cooks the cod along with Cornish oysters and a warm tartare sauce. They both round off the day with a painting challenge on Sennen beach, where they try out their oil painting skills.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieris diving into all kinds of comfort cooking. In Phoenix, a funky joint stackin up barbecue fries and a new spin on chilaquiles. In Boulder, a pizza place loading up the pies and their meatball grinder.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri is diggin into dishes featuring some dynamite dough. In Mesa, a legit Hawaiian joint serving island specialties, including a meaty bun, and in Tucson, a place cookin up a big-time saucy sandwich.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris crisscrossing the nation to grab some international grub. In Dallas, a funky Thai joint serving up noodles, curry and green papaya salad. In Miami, a Latin place dealing in empanadas and a unique plantain sandwich.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieris diving into all kinds of amazing Latin specialties. In Miami, a husband-and-wife team dishing out primo Peruvian sandwiches. In Wilmington, a funky joint cooks up the flavours of Panama.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of

programme.desc: A customer wants to propose to his girlfriend at the bakery and asks Buddy to make an engagement ring box cake.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul returns to Cyprus, the island he called home for six years. He visits its capital city of Nicosia to show viewers the baking culture he knows intimately and loves dearly.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Seafood king Ricks off to Galicia in Spain to sample some rare shellfish. Plus recipes for hake with peppers and saffron, and clams in a marinara sauce.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: This programme looks at the preparation of a spicy shark vindaloo and the making of an authentic dhal with lentils. Plus a visit to Chesapeake Bay in America, where freshwater oysters and crab cakes are the order of the day.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree goes over the top taking some of her favourite recipes to the Nth degree, including mac and cheese stuffed peppers, grilled French toast, fried chicken pizza and funnel cake sundaes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree mourns the loss of her mother-in-law by preparing a variety of dishes for the family based on her favourite meals and classic recipes.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: American Classics
programme.desc: Former food store owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Easy High Low
programme.desc: Former food store owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: A new series kicks off with Cake Week, with challenges includinga a retro classic Technical and a Showstopper birthday cake taking the bakers dozen back to their childhoods.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Everyday
programme.desc: Mary travels to Scotland to enjoy the best of their everyday larder with recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, and shares the joy of venison cooked on a BBQ with chef Tom Kitchin.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: On the next leg of his UK tour, James is joined in glorious Devon by his friend and Michelin-starred chef Simon Hulstone. James visits an extraordinary mushroom farm situated in a bungalow, and makes a tasty steak and mushroom dish. Simon samples some award-winning cheese, which inspires James to make a cheese platter fit for a king. The day ends with a trip to the Art Deco splendour of Burgh Island.

programme.title: James Martins Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by pal and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, and together they set out to explore the delights of Jersey aboard mini motor bikes. James samples the delights of one of Jerseys most famous exports, the Jersey Royal potato, and he uses them to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark Jordan gets a lesson in oyster shucking, and James discovers an island speciality: Jersey black butter, which he uses to create a delicious sweet treat.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: Seafood king Ricks off to Galicia in Spain to sample some rare shellfish. Plus recipes for hake with peppers and saffron, and clams in a marinara sauce.

programme.title: Rick Steins Seafood Odyssey
programme.desc: This programme looks at the preparation of a spicy shark vindaloo and the making of an authentic dhal with lentils. Plus a visit to Chesapeake Bay in America, where freshwater oysters and crab cakes are the order of the day.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree goes over the top taking some of her favourite recipes to the Nth degree, including mac and cheese stuffed peppers, grilled French toast, fried chicken pizza and funnel cake sundaes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree mourns the loss of her mother-in-law by preparing a variety of dishes for the family based on her favourite meals and classic recipes.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a giant Yorkshire pudding with stuffing and roast veg, homemade gnocchi in a cheesy bacon and pea sauce, spicy kipper kedgeree and easy ice cream, all for under a quid a portion.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: American Classics

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.sub-title: Cook Like a Pro: Easy High Low
programme.desc: Former food store owner, business woman, cookbook writer and TV food presenter Ina Garten.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: A new series kicks off with Cake Week, with challenges includinga a retro classic Technical and a Showstopper birthday cake taking the bakers dozen back to their childhoods.

programme.title: Mary Berrys Everyday
programme.desc: Mary travels to Scotland to enjoy the best of their everyday larder with recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, and shares the joy of venison cooked on a BBQ with chef Tom Kitchin.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James culinary tour of Britain continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands. He is joined by top Scottish Michelin starred chef Tom Kitchin. Their journey begins with an incredible tour of the castle grounds complete with awe inspiring views. James kicks off the cooking with his venison with beetroot and blackberries in the castles walled garden. Next Tom Kitchin dusts of his wellies and heads in to the woods for a bit of wild mushroom foraging.

programme.title: James Martins British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Tom are making their way to Edinburgh but before they can do that, James makes a detour to Edradour whisky distillery in Pitlochry where he makes a delicious whisky chicken with wild mushroom and mustard sauce. Once theyve reached Edinburgh, Tom shares his local knowledge by visiting one of his favourite suppliers, Welch Fishmongers. He learns the secret to their amazing smoked salmon which James uses for a salmon mousse with cucumber and dill pickle and melba toast.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy Fieri digs into all kinds of killer grub. In Lansing, an out-of-this-world pizza joint, where the pie comes with a surprise, and in Wilmington, standout sandwiches bring a taste of Philly to the South.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri grabs some off-the-hook seafood and savoury sensations. In Lansing, a food truck dishes out shrimp burgers and a unique potato popper. In Tucson, a Bosnian restaurant fires up legit Eastern European specialities.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri dives into joints where you can literally grab all kinds of grub, starting with a Chicago-style spot in Tucson firing up deep-dish pie and twisted-up breadsticks.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip, Guy Fieri grabs some barbecue and Southern comfort. In Seattle, a funky joint smokes up ribs and dishes out brisket chilli. In Wilmington, a breakfast-brunch spot gets dirty with their biscuits and their meatloaf.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: Hogfish and BBQ shrimp; 15 dozen raw oysters in under an hour.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: A rack of baby back ribs; juicy pulled pork; a six-pound BBQ sandwich with fries.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy makes a canine friend when he is asked to make a cake for an animal shelter. He also challenges his father-in-law to a pizza-making contest.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Children, a Cage, and a Challenge
programme.desc: As well as producing an innovative talking cake for a childrens museum, Buddy is tasked with making a cage-fighting cake.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the North West Regional heat and the scores mean this is a crucial round. Matt Worswick may be the reigning champion but he is not yet assured of a place cooking for the judges and he is feeling the strain.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the three North West competitors are getting creative, producing some of the most original and stunning desserts. All three chefs are working in a hot kitchen, the tension is palpable, and this time one chef will go home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton, and guest judge Lady Claire MacDonald OBE. There are stand out dishes from both chefs making it a difficult contest to judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the finest chefs from Wales are competing to be crowned champion. Two chefs are back for a second time: Phil Carmichael, from Cowbridge, South Wales, is Executive Chef at Berners Tavern in London and Adam Bannister, is chef and co-owner of Slice in his home city of Swansea. Adam was crowed Welsh champion last year so this year Phil is back with something to prove. He plans to go home raising the flag for Wales. The third chef is newcomer Andrew Birch, also from Swansea.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and newcomer Andrew Birch is confident, he is passionate about seafood. He is celebrating the cockle which is harvested near his home city of Swansea. Adam Bannister has created an alter ego, Captain Bannister for his modern take on the old boil in the bag fish dinners and Phil has chosen to revamp the classic prawn cocktail.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Carmichael is serving up his version of an OBE, a refined take on onions, bacon and egg. Adam is planning to serve up Wild boar on a spit, while Andrew turns up the heat when he combines strong Japanese flavours with a traditional roast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and one chef will go home. Phil Carmichael is determined it wont be him but not everything is going to plan when he tackles his Cup of Tea for Maam. One of the accompaniments to his earl grey panna cotta just wont work. He has to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Wales are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, blogger and food writer Rosie Birkett.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers are competing to become regional champion. Daniel Smith, a former head chef for the Roux brothers, now runs his own restaurant in his beloved home county of Norfolk. Andrew Scotts worked with the best in the business, and hes concentrating on telling powerful stories as well as delivering perfect flavours. Danny Gill is a former head chef at Daniel Cliffords two star restaurant Midsummer House.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the chefs are using some of the UKs best seafood: lobster, salmon and Cornish crab. Danny Gill is a chef who likes to use the very latest equipment to create the most intense flavours. Hes going to great lengths to maximise the taste of his cucumber consomme and it is causing a lot of interest in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two chefs are cooking venison. Danny Gill is making a venison wellington, inspired by his Dad, a former RAF chef who has served up Wellington for royalty at many air force banquets. While Andrew Scott has sourced his venison from the Queens Scottish estate, Balmoral.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This week three chefs from the central region are fighting to be crowned champion. One competitor will go home, only the two highest scoring chefs will make it through to the judging chamber. Danny Gill is facing his toughest day yet, choosing to make a staggering eight elements for his imaginative dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central regions two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort, for the very first time. Joining them in the chamber is guest judge Enam Ali MBE, a food entrepreneur who set up The British Curry Awards.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the most exciting talents from London and the South East region are competing for a place in the National Finals. Head chef Mark Froydenlund runs Marcus Wareings two star restaurant. Russell Bateman is head Chef at The Grove in Hertfordshire and Ronnie Murray who heads up the Hix Restaurant group.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and chef Russell Bateman is celebrating the diversity of his beloved home town of London - by combining lobster with jerk spices for an ambitious fish course with seven key elements. Ronnie Murrays taking a risk with an ingredient which is close to the veteran judges heart...and is planning to serve it at three different temperatures warm, deep fried and frozen. And two Michelin starred Mark Froydenlund is planning a novel way to present his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the all-important main course and returning contender Mark Froydenlund is banking on it being his strongest dish. Russell Bateman is creating a beef dish, and Ronnie Murrays mixing a crown of lamb with a curried filling.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the final chance for three of London and South Easts most competitive and talented chefs to get through to the judging chamber, today the veteran judge will send one chef home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head and the two highest scoring chefs from the London and South East region are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort. Joining this week as guest judge is Kevin Gould, an award winning food writer.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel is back for a second attempt at the north east regional title but he is competing against two young newcomers, Tommy Banks and Chris Archer. Last year, Mini went home after the dessert course, so he is hoping to keep better control of his nerves this year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and two chefs, Tommy and Mini, have very similar ingredients - both are cooking mackerel. But Mini is trying an original beetroot sorbet while Tommy is using wild woodruff and oyster leaf from his own garden. Chris Archer has chosen the king of the sea, turbot, but there is controversy in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the north east region and overnight there has been a dramatic development. It is a tough and emotional day for the chefs as they react to the news but they battle on in a bid to present a perfect dish with venison and wagyu-angus beef - the meat of the day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is a highly emotional day for one of the chefs as he prepares a dish which is inspired by and a tribute to his own great Briton, his late grandfather. But all is not going to plan and the chef ends up making one key element three times in a bid to achieve perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton for the very first time. Joining them as guest judge is John Williams MBE, the executive head chef at The Ritz, the only hotel to hold a royal warrant for banqueting.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is Chris McGowans third time however, he is facing tough competition from Mark Abbott, head chef at Daniel Cliffords 2 Michelin star restaurant, Midsummer House, and also from Eddie Attwell who has just completed a year as head chef at Ardtara Country House in Upperlands.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mark Abbott is pushing himself again plating up a complex fish course of the very finest produce. He is calling it Shellsational but at the last minute he forgets a key element. Returning chef Chris McGowan is refining cod and chips and Eddie Attwell is getting classical with the king of the sea, turbot.The repartee is relentless as the chefs jostle for top place on the leader board. Once again there is plenty of emotion as the veteran chef delivers the scores.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and returning Chef Chris McGowan is feeling confident but part of his dish involves making his own venison sausages to serve with a venison rack, and getting it all done in time is straining his emotions. Eddie Attwell is also serving the meat but with a traditional Northern Irish dish called Dock pudding. Mark Abbott is pushing himself again, he is using wagyu beef and serving it with tongue and corn cooked in a variety of ways.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking their desserts and one chef is on course for a historic score, he could beat his boss - a veteran of GBM - and achieve more marks than anyone has done throughout the history of the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, restaurant critic and Editor-at-Large of the Independent, Amol Rajan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to Londons Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its day two of the national finals, and the eight champions cook their fish courses. This years challenge has been to produce dishes that are a lasting legacy to the New Elizabethan Age. They must showcase the transformation in British cuisine during the Queens historic 64 year reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day, the one course every chef wants to cook at the banquet. Judging the chefs dishes are Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton and guest judge John Williams MBE, executive chef of The Ritz.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition, and the last chance for the eight champions to get one of their dishes to the banquet for Queen Elizabeth lls Great Britons. Judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton will judge the dessert course and then decide on the banquet menu to be served at the Houses of Parliament. Joining them in the judging chamber is Grace Dent, restaurant critic, journalist and author.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queens historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. Hes taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protege of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Maam Has Seen Some Trotters, a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dans `For All Those Sleepless Nights pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Dannys `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. Davids dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiance, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewes milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwens Chocolate. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital AandE during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again, but will they take on board their veterans advice as they serve their `Feast To Say Thank You?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Banquet champion Selin Kiazim, a rising star of the London scene renowned for her Turkish Cypriot fusion flavours, is back hoping to join the elite group of chefs who have made it to the banquet twice. She is up against James Cochran whos worked at some of Londons toughest kitchens including 2 Michelin starred `The Ledbury and Scott Goss from `The Twenty Six in Tunbridge Wells, renowned for his use of local Kent ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs do battle for the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen is tense. Lobster is catch of the day for James whose dish is Caribbean-inspired. Scott is attempting to elevate a classic fish pie, dedicated to his mum who worked as a pharmacist in the NHS for 23 years. And Selin is taking a risk with a complex three-part fish dish featuring sardines, scallops and langoustines, as a nod to the purported health benefits of a Mediterranean diet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scott is showcasing rare breed Tamworth pork to say thank you to the rare breed of people who work for the NHS. Selin is serving a complex duck dish using ingredients from all around the world to honour `Our Global NHS. And James is presenting five cuts of goat cooked in five different ways, for his bold dish `Under The Knife.

programme.title: Ghost Ships

programme.title: Britains Secret Islands

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: How (Not) to Get Rid of a Body
programme.desc: Las Vegas detectives find themselves on the hunt of their careers. They try to track down those responsible for the horrific murder of a young woman after her body turns up in a concrete and wooden box in the middle of the desert.

programme.title: A Body in the Basement
programme.desc: In 1998, new homeowners renovating their basement discover a body. As police investigate, they learn it belongs to teenager Christopher Denoyer, who was reported missing by his family in the 80s.

programme.title: Cabin in the Woods
programme.desc: A young woman cosies up with her new boyfriend at his remote cabin but has no idea who he really is or what hes done. When he reveals his violent side, she must dig deep in the hopes of surviving every hellish scenario he throws at her.

programme.title: Police 911 Michigan
programme.desc: On September 6, 2024, aspiring rapper Michael Munson is found shot in his car, with cash and drugs pointing to a motive. Witnesses flee in a grey Charger, which later appears at another shooting. Detectives suspect a link - but can they prove it?

programme.title: The Perfect Suspect
programme.desc: After a young couple is found murdered and mutilated on the outskirts of a small Ohio town, police work tirelessly to build a case against a suspect, but it will take more than 25 years for the terrible truth to emerge.

programme.title: The 1990s: The Deadliest Decade
programme.desc: When Lisa Carlson is found killed in her home, its thought that robbers surprised her during an intimate moment and killed her. But the scene was staged.

programme.title: The 1990s: The Deadliest Decade

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When an old colleague of DCI Barnabys is found murdered, a sex scandal at Causton police station 16 years previously comes back to haunt him.
